description_long: This is a hand axe featuring a robust, slightly curved wooden handle with a visible grain pattern and a dark, weathered finish. The handle is joined to a broad, wedge-shaped metal blade that has a pronounced cutting edge and a flat poll on the opposite side. The blade is affixed to the handle using a traditional socket, with the metal wrapping around the top of the wood. The axe head is dark gray, showing signs of wear and use, and the blade edge is slightly beveled for chopping efficiency. The overall design is utilitarian, suitable for tasks such as chopping wood or clearing brush in urban parks or gardens. No visible branding or decorative elements are present, emphasizing its practical, workmanlike character.
description: A utilitarian hand axe with a curved wooden handle and a broad, weathered metal blade, designed for chopping tasks. The blade is socket-mounted and shows signs of use.
description_view_0: Side view showing the full length of the wooden handle and the broad, wedge-shaped metal blade.
description_view_1: Front view highlighting the sharp edge of the blade and the narrow profile of the handle.
description_view_2: Opposite side view, mirroring the handle and blade profile seen in view 0.
description_view_3: Rear view displaying the end of the handle and the back of the axe head.
attribute_handle_type: Curved wooden handle with a round cross-section, visible grain, and a slightly angled grip for ergonomic use.
attribute_blade_design: Single broad wedge-shaped steel blade with a beveled cutting edge and a flat poll; no decorative features.
attribute_mounting_style: Traditional socket mounting with the blade wrapped around the top of the wooden handle.
